msgs.text:
I read the problem statement and the full prior-turn log. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma = tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and constants modulo M=85667662080.The prior log convincingly refutes the early candidates; the only candidate still alive is the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äì24.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with œÄ=œÄ2 and Œ∑=2œÄ on tame inertia,r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1,where œÄ‚â°40491355905,‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175,‚àíŒ∑=‚àí2œÄ ‚â°50932630783(modM).My new contribution is a uniform version of Turn 24‚Äôs unramified-cubic Smith calculation. Turn 24 checked f=3, where œÉœÄ=S1/3, and found that the sign-kernel torsion is C4 exactly for the candidate‚Äôs q=1/3. I generalized that computation to every unramified degree f.

Let f=2a m, m odd, and let s‚â°œÄ(modf),s‚â°1(mod2a),s‚â°0(modm).In the pro-2 kernel above the unramified degree-f quotient, put S=œÉf. ThenœÉœÄ=œÉsSq,q=f1‚àís‚ààZ2.After setting œÑ=1, the candidate relation gives Reidemeister‚ÄìSchreier relatorsri=xiSŒΩi‚àíqxi‚àísS q‚àíŒΩi+œµiyi+1S‚àíœµiyi‚àí1,i‚ààZ/fZ,where indices are modulo f,ŒΩi={0,‚àí1,i‚â•s,i<s,œµi={1,0,i=f‚àí1,iÓÄÝ=f‚àí1.The ordinary abelianization of this unramified kernel has the expected relationsXi+Xi‚àís+Yi+1‚àíYi=0,so the Sq -term is invisible there. It becomes visible in the sign kernel.

Take the sign/orientation characterŒ∫(Xi)=Œ∫(Yi)=1,Œ∫(S)=0,and let Vf=kerŒ∫. Using transversal {1,x0}, I wrote the abelianized Reidemeister‚ÄìSchreier matrix Mf (q,s) for Vf. It has 2f rows and 4f+1 columns.

The row/column reduction over Z2 gives the normal formMf (q,s)‚àºdiag( 2f‚àí1¬Ýtimes1,‚Ä¶,1,2(fq+s+1)),with the remaining 2f+1 columns free.Concretely, the unit pivots can be seen from the (2f‚àí1)√ó(2f‚àí1) minor using columns{xi(0):1‚â§i<f}‚à™{yi(0):0‚â§i<f‚àí1}‚à™{xs(1)},and all rows except the last; its determinant is ¬±1. Adding the column S(0)and the last row gives determinant ¬±2(fq+s+1).

For the actual candidate,q=f1‚àís,so fq+s+1=(1‚àís)+s+1=2,and therefore the unique non-unit Smith factor is 2(fq+s+1)=4.

Thus, uniformly for every unramified degree f,Vfab‚âÖZ22f+1‚äïC4.

This subsumes several earlier tests at once:f34612s0139 q=(1‚àís)/f1/30‚àí1/3‚àí2/3 2(fq+s+1)4444 So Turn 24‚Äôs f=3 calculation was not an isolated coincidence: the Sq -term selected by œÉœÄ gives exactly the C4 sign-kernel torsion for all unramified f. This is a real strengthening of the orientation evidence for the live candidate.It also clarifies what is and is not proved. The live word now has strong evidence at the finite-tame pro-2 kernel level: correct Frattini module, nondegenerate cup form, correct sign class, correct C4 sign-kernel torsion in all unramified degrees, and the dynamic tame-inertia correction that fixed the (91,12) obstruction. But this still does not prove the full absolute Galois presentation. The remaining gap is the same structural one: a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem must identify the full filtered tame action, including all Sq -terms and œÑ‚àí2œÄ -terms, with the arithmetic action over every finite tame subextension.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I generalized Turn 24‚Äôs unramified cubic Smith computation to arbitrary unramified degree f. For the sign kernel Vf=kerŒ∫, the abelianized Reidemeister‚ÄìSchreier matrix has Z2 -Smith form with one non-unit factor 2(fq+s+1). For the candidate‚Äôs q=(1‚àís)/f, this factor is 4, henceVfab‚âÖZ22f+1‚äïC4 for every f.

GAP: This is still not a complete proof of the presentation. The missing step is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem matching the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered Frobenius and tame-inertia actions with the arithmetic actions for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Extend this Smith-normal-form calculation from unramified f to genuinely tamely ramified quotients (e,f), especially the sign kernel above nontrivial odd tame-inertia character blocks, and isolate the exact analogue of the scalar 2(fq+s+1) there.[BREAKTHROUGH]
